Installing and Configuring NetSim 2

  • Installing NetSim 2
  • Configuring NetSim 2
  • Configuring Xbox Consoles to use NetSim

Installing NetSim 2

NetSim 2 must be installed after you install the XDK. The XDK installation program creates an icon for the NetSim 2 Setup program in the Start menu of your development PC. Find and click Microsoft Xbox XDK in your Start menu, and then click Xbox Live. Next, click Setup Microsoft Network Simulation Tool 2 for Xbox.

Configuring NetSim 2

The NetSim 2 Setup program installs NetSim 2 and handles most setup issues. It then starts NetSim 2. The first time NetSim 2 runs, it displays a dialog box stating that it is not configured correctly. It gives you the choice of configuring it at that time or canceling. If you choose to configure NetSim 2 immediately, the program displays its configuration dialog box.

The NetSim 2 configuration dialog box contains 3 tabbed pages. The Consoles tab displays a page asking you to input the number of Xbox consoles you want to use. It also requests the IP address of the first console. By default, the starting address is 10.0.0.2. Depending on the network configuration at your location, you may need to use an address range that is higher than the default. If use of NetSim seems to be causing packets to be blocked, try using 10.0.100.2 as the starting address.

Note    If you specify a large number of consoles (100 or more), NetSim 2 may appear to hang for up to 10 minutes when you start the simulation. However, this is not the case. It takes NetSim 2 a while to configure large groups of consoles.

The page displayed by the Internet tab closely resembles the dialog box Windows displays when you examine the TCP/IP properties for your PC's LAN connection. It works in exactly the same manner.

The Logging tab of the configuration dialog box lets you enable or disable event logging. If you enable logging, the page also gives you the opportunity to specify a name for the log file.

If you later want to update the configuration, you can do so by first clicking Options on the main menu of NetSim 2 and then clicking Configuration. NetSim 2 presents you with the same configuration dialog box you used in the initial configuration of the program.

Configuring Xbox Consoles to use NetSim

NetSim 2 can support up to 256 client Xbox consoles. This limitation derives from the IP range (10.0.0.2 to 10.0.255.2) that client consoles can have. Be sure to assign each Xbox console a unique IP address in this range; use the Title IP address field in the XDK Launcher Network Settings screen. For each client whose IP is 10.0.x.2, that client's Gateway and Primary DNS should be configured to 10.0.x.1. Each client's Subnet mask should be 255.255.255.0, which forces all packets through NetSim before going anywhere else (such as to another Xbox console on the same network). Other fields in the Network Settings screen are not used by NetSim.
